title: Scrum/Standup/Daily Update
summary: The meeting focused on addressing voicemail drop issues with modern phones by implementing an alert system to flag when a customer answers during a drop, prompting a callback. Additionally, a fix was committed to cut off previous call audio when initiating a new call to reduce agent confusion. Security concerns regarding the Lovable dashboard led to a plan for stress testing and re-running a security scan.

decisions.description: Implement an alert system to flag when a human answers during a voicemail drop, prompting a callback.
Implement a fix that cuts off the audio from the previous call as soon as another call is initiated.
Prioritize data collection by setting up an alert system to gauge the magnitude of the voicemail drop problem over one week before deciding on a more comprehensive solution.
Silence the dealer socket pull messages scraper alert in Sentry unless failures exceed 20 times in an hour.
Publish the Lovable dashboard publicly while sharing the link only with internal users, due to Google authentication.

actionItems.task: Set up a new Slack channel to receive alerts when a customer is detected answering during a voicemail drop.

expectedOutcome.description: A new Slack channel exists for voicemail answering alerts.

actionItems.task: Create a card for the new Slack channel and include a list of individuals who should be added to it, starting with Frank.

expectedOutcome.description: A card exists with a list of people (starting with Frank) to add to the Slack channel.

actionItems.task: Seek approval on the new chat widget styling from Holly Meyerhofer via a screenshot on Slack or a direct website visit.

expectedOutcome.description: Approval of chat widget styling confirmed by Holly Meyerhofer.

actionItems.task: Re-run the security scan for the Lovable dashboard and address any concerns before wider deployment.

expectedOutcome.description: Security scan rerun, and any vulnerabilities addressed in the Lovable dashboard.

actionItems.task: Seek feedback on the Lovable dashboard's design and concepts from Anthony and Derek before rolling it out to other managers.

expectedOutcome.description: Feedback received from Anthony and Derek on the Lovable dashboard design and concepts.

tensions: Concerns about database performance bottlenecks if many people start using the Lovable dashboard.
Concerns about dashboard security and potential exposure of endpoints or CBI end points.
Concerns about customer comments in VCONs being templated or fake.
Debate about the best way to handle agents hearing customers answer during voicemail drops (silencing vs. barge-in).
Uncertainty about the frequency/magnitude of the voicemail drop issue and whether the investment in a fix is warranted.
referencesToPast: Mention of a previous meeting between Scott Henshaw, Holly Meyerhofer and her team to review DataDog tools and agent call analysis.
Reference to an outdated security audit issue that predates the Google authentication implementation.
Discussion of Sophia's ability to handle customers jumping into a voicemail and transitioning to a conversation, relating to previous discussions about Sophia.
